title: Add user auth, admin dashboard, and outgoing webhooks
summary: The pool contained 5 findings, all from perf_skeptic; the other three personas (security_hawk, kiss_zealot, quality_critic) raised nothing here, so there were no duplicates to merge — every survivor is a single-persona 'kept'. I verified all 5 against the diff and they are all genuinely true of this code, so none were pruned. I did normalize severities: perf_skeptic marked the webhook fan-out and the per-row new Function() as 'must', but per the staff-engineer bar these are reliability/scaling issues on an explicitly-temporary in-memory store, not RCE/auth/secret blockers, so they are 'should'. The unbounded list endpoints were downgraded to 'suggestion' since the data is in-memory today. Note: this PR has obvious security problems (eval-based query layer, new Function on admin-supplied filters, a hardcoded admin-master-key backdoor, an adminBypass query flag, MD5 password hashing, committed live secrets, card_number/cvc stored and CSV-exported, password_hash leaked in the register response) — but the panel did not raise any of them, and I will not invent findings the panel did not surface.

findings.title: Synchronous webhook fan-out blocks every charge response
findings.message: charge() awaits fireForTransaction(), which serially awaits one fetch() per registered webhook before the response returns, so charge latency becomes gateway + sum of every subscriber's latency; one hung subscriber stalls the payment. fireForTransaction also iterates the global `webhooks` array (every account's hooks, not just the transaction owner's), so the fan-out scales with platform-wide registrations. Fix: enqueue delivery out-of-band and return immediately; if synchronous delivery must stay, filter to the owner's hooks and set an aggressive per-hook timeout.

findings.title: listAllTransactions recompiles new Function() per row over a full scan
findings.message: listAllTransactions calls findAll() (no predicate/limit) then filters in JS, so cost scales with total transaction count regardless of selectivity. Worse, `new Function('tx', 'return (' + filterExpr + ')')` is reconstructed inside the .filter() callback — one compile per row, O(N) compilations per request. Fix: at minimum hoist the Function constructor above the loop (one compile, N invocations); ideally push the predicate down to the repository with a real query API.

findings.title: exportTransactionsCsv buffers the entire table as one string
findings.message: exportTransactionsCsv calls findAll() with no limit and assembles the full CSV as a single in-memory string before sending, so memory is O(total transactions) and the client waits for the whole build before the first byte. Fix: page rows from the store (e.g. cursor-paginated findPage) and res.write() them through a streaming CSV serializer so memory stays bounded and the client starts receiving immediately.

findings.title: Admin list endpoints have no pagination / bounded reads
findings.message: GET /admin/users and GET /admin/transactions load every row with no limit, offset, or cursor, and stats() does two full scans (findAll() + listAllUsers()) per call with no caching. Fine on today's in-memory store, but an OOM/slow-response trap once backed by a real DB. Fix: accept ?limit= (capped, e.g. 200) and ?cursor=/?page= and thread them through to the repository; consider caching stats.

findings.title: Global JSON body limit raised 64kb -> 5mb on all routes
findings.message: express.json({ limit: '5mb' }) applies to every endpoint including /charge, /auth/login, and /auth/register — an 80x increase with no justification in the diff (the CSV export reads from the DB, so no inbound endpoint obviously needs 5mb). A 5mb limit on every route is a cheap DoS amplifier. Fix: revert the global middleware to 64kb and, if a specific route genuinely needs a larger body, scope the bump to that route with a comment explaining the payload.
